info.title: Count Public API
info.description: The Count public API allows you to interact with your Count workspace programmatically.

post.description: Create an agent in an existing or new canvas. Once created, agents will continue their work autonomously.

context.description: The context to create the agent in. This context determines which data sources the agent has access to. You must have an analyst role in this context.

anyOf.description: Create an agent in a canvas context
Create an agent in a project context

context_key.description: The unique identifier for the context.

query.description: An initial instruction for the agent

example.query: Who are our biggest customers this quarter?

url.description: A link to the new agent

canvas_key.description: The unique identifier for the canvas

agent_key.description: The unique identifier for an agent within a canvas

get.description: Fetch metadata about a canvas. The service account must have at least a viewer role for this canvas.

parameters.name: canvas_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the canvas

get.description: List all visible canvases in the workspace accessible with this service account. Trashed canvases and those that the service account is not permitted to view are not returned.

canvases.description: The list of canvases in the workspace.

get.description: Get the metadata and contents of a catalog. The service account must be permitted to view the chosen catalog.

parameters.name: catalog_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the catalog

parameters.name: files
parameters.description: Include this parameter to request the catalog file contents.

anyOf.description: Return the raw files from the catalog repository.
Return both the raw and generated catalog files (for example, third-party semantic layer definitions converted to Count Metrics files).

parameters.name: definitions
parameters.description: Include this parameter to return the catalog definitions in a structured data format.

anyOf.description: Return the catalog definitions in JSON format.

truncated.description: Whether the file contents are truncated - this can occur if the file is particularly large.

datasets.description: The dataset definitions in this catalog.

from.description: The name of the view that this dataset is based on.

views.description: The views included in this dataset.

join.description: The views joined into this dataset.

items.description: A joined view within a dataset.

view.description: The name of the view to join.

alias.description: An alias for the joined view.

label.description: A display label for the joined view.
The display label for this dataset.

path.description: The file path of the dataset definition.

anyOf.description: A definition converted from a LookML source.
A definition converted from a OSI source.
A definition converted from a Snowflake source.
A native Count Metrics definition that extends another.
A LookML-converted definition that extends another.
A OSI-converted definition that extends another.
A Snowflake-converted definition that extends another.

sha.description: The commit SHA of the catalog.

views.description: The view definitions in this catalog.

items.description: A field definition within a view.

expression.description: The SQL expression for this field.

type.description: The data type of the field.

aggregates.description: The aggregate functions available for this field.

format.description: The display format specification for this field.

group.description: The group this field belongs to.

label.description: The display label for this field.

primary_key.description: Whether this field is a primary key.

timeframes.description: The timeframe functions available for this field.

source.description: The data source for this view.

anyOf.description: The unique identifier for the connection

items.description: A cell dependency for a view source.

connection.description: The connection to use for this dependency.

anyOf.description: The unique identifier for the connection

url.description: A URL associated with this source.

connection.description: The unique identifier for the connection

query.description: The query expression for the table.

label.description: The display label for this view.

schedule.description: A cron expression for the cache refresh schedule.

wait_for_cache.description: Whether to wait for the cache to be populated before returning results.

path.description: The file path of the view definition.

view.description: The name of the view to join.

anyOf.description: A definition converted from a LookML source.
A definition converted from a OSI source.
A definition converted from a Snowflake source.
A native Count Metrics definition that extends another.
A LookML-converted definition that extends another.
A OSI-converted definition that extends another.
A Snowflake-converted definition that extends another.

invalid.description: Whether the catalog definitions contain validation errors.

get.description: List all catalogs in the workspace accessible with this service account.

catalogs.description: The list of catalogs in the workspace

get.description: Fetch metadata about a connection. The service account must have at least a viewer role for this connection.

parameters.name: connection_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connection

get.description: List all connections in the workspace accessible with this service account.

connections.description: The list of connections in the workspace

post.description: Trigger a refresh of the connection metadata (for example, dbt manifests). The refresh operation may take several minutes to complete, depending on the size of the metadata artifacts. If a branch name is not specified, the metadata will be updated from the default branch.

parameters.name: connection_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connection

parameters.name: branch
parameters.description: The GitHub branch name to refresh.

post.description: Trigger a refresh of the connection schema. The refresh operation may take several minutes to complete, depending on the size of the database.

parameters.name: connection_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connection

get.description: Fetch metadata about a project. The service account must have at least a viewer role for this project.

parameters.name: project_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the project

get.description: List all projects in the workspace accessible with this service account.

projects.description: The list of projects in the workspace

post.description: Generate a CSV file export of existing query results. Once the CSV file has been created, a URL to the file will be available when fetching this query again.

parameters.name: job_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the query job

parameters.name: wait_for
parameters.description: Wait up to this many seconds for the CSV file to be created (to a maximum of 60)

url.description: The URL of the converted CSV file (if available)

anyOf.description: A signed URL that can be used to download query results. This URL expires after 1 hour, after which a new URL can be requested using this route.
If the CSV file has not yet been created

get.description: Get the status of a previously-submitted query

parameters.name: job_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the query job

parameters.name: num_rows
parameters.description: The number of rows to return in the result preview, up to 1,000.

get.description: Get the most recent queries for a canvas cell, ordered from newest to oldest.

parameters.name: canvas_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the canvas

parameters.name: cell_key
parameters.description: The unique identifier for a cell in a canvas

parameters.name: num_rows
parameters.description: The number of rows to return in the result preview, up to 1,000.

post.description: Run a new query in a given context - one of the `sql` or `structured` parameters must be present. A reference to the created query job is returned. If the same query is submitted multiple times, the same job reference may be returned if caching is enabled.

context.description: The context to run the query in. This context determines which data sources the query has access to. The service account must have an analyst role in this context.

anyOf.description: Run a query in a canvas context
Run a query in a project context

context_key.description: The unique identifier for the context.

source.description: The data source to run the query against. The source must be available in the chosen context.

source_key.description: The connection to run the query on. Use `null` to mean DuckDB.

anyOf.description: The unique identifier for the connection

sql.description: A SQL query definition, which can only be used to query a database connection source

structured.description: A structured query definition, which can only be used to query a catalog source. Read more about the expression syntax [here](https://learn.count.co/data-exploration-and-problem-solving/visual-and-low-code-cells/calculations-in-visuals-and-low-code-cells), which is a reduced subset of Postgres-like SQL.

dataset.description: The name of the dataset to query. This should always be the name of a dataset, not a view.

selects.description: A list of expressions to select

view.description: The name of the view that the field comes from.

expression.description: The name of a field, or an expression for a calculated field. Names of fields should always be qualified with view names, in case there are multiple fields with the same name in different views.

filters.description: Optional filters to apply to the result.

combine.description: How multiple filters should be combined

anyOf.description: Combine using the AND boolean operation.
Combine using the OR boolean operation

view.description: The name of the view that the field comes from

cache_strategy.description: Define the caching behaviour for this query.

anyOf.description: Return cached results if available, otherwise run a new query

job_key.description: The unique identifier for the query job

compiled_sql.description: The SQL query that was constructed (if available).

get.description: List all of the members in the workspace. This list includes guests, but does not include service accounts.

members.description: The list of workspace members and their roles

key.description: The unique identifier for the user

name.description: The display name for the user

email.description: The email address for the user

role.description: The role of a workspace member. This role may be affected by group memberships.

get.description: Search for canvases in the workspace by title or content.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The search query to match against canvas content (must be non-empty)

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: The offset to start from (for pagination)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: The field to sort by

anyOf.description: How relevant the search result is

parameters.name: project_keys
parameters.description: Filter results to only these projects. Multiple projects should be represented as a comma-separated list

parameters.name: title_only
parameters.description: Only search in canvas titles

parameters.name: created_by
parameters.description: Filter results to canvases created by these users. Multiple users should be represented as a comma-separated list

key.description: The unique identifier for the canvas

name.description: The display name for the canvas

project_key.description: The unique identifier for the project

project_name.description: The display name for the project

created_at.description: The creation date of the canvas.

created_by.description: The unique identifier for the user

modified_at.description: When the canvas was last modified (if any).

anyOf.description: The unique identifier for the user

preview.description: A preview image URL for the canvas

object_results.description: The list of search results within objects in this canvas

key.description: The unique identifier for an object in a canvas

text.description: The text contents of the object

more_results.description: Whether there are more results available

NullableDateTime.description: An ISO 8601 date-time string, or null.

SuccessResponse.description: Base response envelope for all successful API responses.

key.description: The unique identifier for the canvas

name.description: The display name for the canvas

created_at.description: The creation date of the canvas.

modified_at.description: When the canvas was last modified (if any).

anyOf.description: The unique identifier for the user

creator.description: The unique identifier for the user

permissions.description: The list of actors with assigned permissions for this canvas.

items.description: A permission assigned to an actor for a canvas.

key.description: The unique identifier for the actor.

permitted_actions.description: The actions you are allowed to perform in this canvas

create_agent.description: Are you able to create an agent or not.

preview.description: A preview image URL for the canvas

key.description: The unique identifier for the project

name.description: The display name for the project

created_at.description: The creation date of the project.

modified_at.description: When the project was last modified (if any).

permitted_actions.description: The actions you are allowed to perform in this project

create_canvas.description: Are you able to create a canvas or not.

run_query.description: Are you able to query data sources in this project or not.

permissions.description: The list of actors with assigned permissions for this project.

items.description: A permission assigned to an actor for a project.

key.description: The unique identifier for the actor.

sources.description: The data sources that are accessible through this project.

source_key.description: The unique identifier for the data source.

datasets.description: For catalog sources, the explicit list of exposed datasets (if any).

agent_context.description: Agent-specific context for this project (if any).

Connection.description: A data connection, usually a SQL database

key.description: The unique identifier for the connection

name.description: The display name for the connection

created_at.description: The creation date of the connection.

modified_at.description: When the connection was last modified (if any).

projects.description: Projects where this connection is available.

items.description: The unique identifier for the project

color.description: The default color for this connection

last_schema_refresh_at.description: When the schema was last refreshed.

schema_refresh_frequency.description: How often the schema is refreshed (as a cron expression).

key.description: The unique identifier for the catalog

name.description: The display name for the catalog

created_at.description: The creation date of the catalog.

modified_at.description: When the catalog was last modified (if any).

permissions.description: The list of actors with assigned permissions for this catalog.

items.description: A permission assigned to an actor for a catalog.

key.description: The unique identifier for the actor.

sources.description: The connections that this catalog can access.

connection_key.description: The unique identifier for the connection

connection_name.description: The display name for the connection

agent_context.description: Agent-specific context for this catalog (if any).

job_key.description: The unique identifier for the query job

status.description: The current status of the job

anyOf.description: The query has been submitted, but the results are not ready to be fetched yet.
The query has finished, and results are ready to be fetched.

error.description: The error returned during query execution.

results.description: URLs for query results in available formats (if completed).

items.description: The URL for this result format.

format.description: The format of the query results at this URL

anyOf.description: A [Parquet file](https://arrow.apache.org/docs/python/parquet.html) compressed using the zstd method.

url.description: A signed URL that can be used to download query results. This URL expires after 1 hour, after which a new URL can be requested using this route.

result_preview.description: A preview of the first rows of the results in CSV format (if completed). By default up to 50 rows are returned, unless otherwise specified in the num_rows option.

columns.description: The columns in the result set

anyOf.description: A numeric type, including integers, floats and high-precision types.

duration.description: The total query duration in milliseconds

num_rows.description: The number of rows in the result set

row_limit.description: The row limit applied when extracting the results from the database

byte_limit.description: The byte limit applied when extracting the results from the database

sql.description: The executed SQL for this query (if available)

Error.description: Error response returned by all API endpoints on failure.

ClientError.description: Client error. Possible status codes include: 400 (invalid request), 401 (missing or invalid API key), 403 (insufficient permissions), 404 (resource not found), 429 (rate limit exceeded).

bearerAuth.description: API key authentication. Provide your API key as a Bearer token.
